## Dennis Buzukja -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (4 UFC fights with partial splits; 602-day layoff triggers stale-data discount per Rule 2.3 and layoff penalty per Rule 9.2). Archetype: **Counter/Pressure Striker** — SLpM 4.21 career, moderate volume, no takedown offense to speak of (0.63 TD/15min, 27% acc). Direction tag: **FORWARD** (initiates exchanges based on style and KO patterns).
- **Striking output collapsed in most recent fight**: Last-3 SLpM average is 2.58 vs. career 4.21 — the Marshall fight dragged the average down catastrophically (0.4 SLpM in a 3-round decision loss). Accuracy held around 37–45% across last 3, roughly FLAT vs. career 43%.
- **Defensive liability confirmed**: Career SApM 4.82 — absorbs significantly more than he lands. Strike defense at 48% is below the UFC average. Per Rule 3.3, this qualifies as a **defensive decay concern** even without a formal CHIN CRACKED flag.
- **KO vulnerability is real and recent**: KO/TKO loss to Jamall Emmers in R1 at 0:49 — a Round 1 stoppage against a non-elite opponent. Per Rule 6.2 criteria: (1) R1 KO loss in last 3 years ✓, (2) early stoppage (R1) ✓. **CHIN CRACKED flag is borderline** — technically 1 KO loss in the last 3 years in recent fight history shown, but the R1 nature and defensive metrics together warrant flagging as **CHIN AT RISK**.
- **602-day layoff (Rule 9.2)**: Applies a **−12% win probability penalty** AND career stats are treated as suspect. His last fight was August 2024 — over 20 months out. Ring rust is a significant factor here, especially given he's returning to face a KO finisher.
- **Win methods are sparse and inconsistent**: 1 KO (R3 vs. Matthews) and 1 decision in last 6 fights, with 4 losses. The KO win came in R3 after sustained early pressure; the losses have come via decision and KO — not the profile of a finisher, but a fighter who can be walked down and stopped.
<!--END-->

<!--F2_PROFILE-->
## Marcio Barbosa -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: LOW** — Only 1 UFC fight on record (August 2025 vs. Damon Wilson, KO/TKO R1). Per Rule 2.2, all stat analysis is limited to that single fight plus pre-UFC record. **Confidence cap: 60% maximum** per Rule 13.4. Archetype: **Power Striker / Volume Striker hybrid** — 8.27 SLpM in sole UFC appearance with 47% accuracy; all wins tracked in recent history are by KO/TKO. Direction tag: **FORWARD** (high-output pressure style based on the KO pattern).
- **Extreme striking volume in UFC debut**: 8.27 SLpM in his one UFC fight is elite-tier output — top 5% of the division by volume. However, this is a **1-fight sample against Damon Wilson**, an unranked opponent. Per Rule 2.4, no "trending" conclusions are possible; treat as a single data point.
- **No takedown offense whatsoever**: 0.00 TD/15min, 0% TD accuracy across UFC stats. He is a pure striker — no grappling threat identified. This simplifies the matchup dynamic considerably: this fight lives and dies on the feet.
- **Defensive numbers raise flags**: Career SApM 7.56 (absorbs nearly as much as he lands at 8.27) and only 50% strike defense. Both numbers are from a single fight against a limited opponent — but the "trading" pattern is real. This is not a defensive-minded fighter.
- **17-2 pre-UFC record** with strong finish-rate: The 17-2 record with finishes being the primary win method signals legitimate KO power. However, per Rule 2.4, the quality of pre-UFC opposition is unknown — treat the record as LOW tier without verified opponent quality context.
- **235-day layoff (Rule 9.2)**: Standard turnaround — no layoff penalty applies. Active, in-form fighter heading into this matchup.
<!--END-->

<!--HEAD2HEAD-->
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

**Structural read — Matrix outcome (Rule 5.1):** This is a **Volume Striker vs. Power/Volume Striker** matchup between two FORWARD fighters. Per the matrix (Rule 4.4): when two FORWARDs meet, expect brawling, early finish potential, fade decision props. The decisive variable here is not style symmetry — it is the **massive asymmetry in form, activity, and raw output**. Barbosa enters with 8.27 SLpM and a fresh KO finish; Buzukja enters with a 602-day layoff, a KO/TKO loss in R1 on his recent record, documented defensive decay (48% strike defense, 4.82 SApM), and a striking output that cratered to 0.4 SLpM in his most recent outing. The structural matrix gives no clean archetype edge — but the **intangible and durability stack on Buzukja is severe**.

**The critical exchange:** Both fighters lack takedown offense (Barbosa 0 TDs, Buzukja 0.63/15min at 27% accuracy — neither will meaningfully change the fight's location). This will be decided on the feet. Barbosa's volume advantage is enormous: 8.27 SLpM vs. Buzukja's career 4.21 — and Buzukja's last-3 average has dropped to 2.58. The pace mismatch is the fight. Buzukja needs to land accurately and find a KO before Barbosa's volume overwhelms him. His own KO power is demonstrated (R3 KO vs. Matthews), but his chin is the question. The Emmers R1 KO at 0:49 is the critical data point — he was stopped by a fighter who is not an elite finisher, which is a CHIN AT RISK signal under Rule 6.2.

**Net intangible delta (Rule 9.11):** Buzukja carries: −12% (602-day layoff, Rule 9.2) + CHIN AT RISK discount (~−5% contextual) = approximately **−17% total intangible penalty**. Barbosa carries: no active intangible penalties. Net delta: **Barbosa +17% from intangibles alone**, before any style or stats adjustment. The flip test (Rule 15.1): if Buzukja's layoff was due to injury with a full camp return, soften the penalty; if Barbosa's one-fight UFC sample turns out to be against a fighter with significantly better defense, discount his KO probability. Neither of those conditions changes the directional read materially here.

**Flip conditions (Rule 15.1):** (1) If Buzukja's layoff involved a complete stylistic overhaul at a new camp and the 0.4 SLpM vs. Marshall is explained by gameplan rather than ability decline, soften the fade. (2) If Damon Wilson had above-average UFC striking defense and Barbosa's 8.27 SLpM is accurate against quality opposition, upgrade the KO probability further. (3) If Barbosa's pre-UFC record reveals faded opposition, the raw 17-2 record should be discounted further — but it doesn't change who is ring-sharp vs. ring-rusty here.
<!--END-->

<!--ENDINGS-->
## 3-5 Most Likely Fight Endings (Ranked by Probability)

**Section 8 math basis:**
- Barbosa base KO rate: ~75% of wins by KO (pre-UFC + UFC), use 0.65 as estimated base_KO_rate (conservative given LOW data tier)
- Buzukja Str. Def.: 48% → (1 − 0.48) = 0.52
- Chin modifier for Buzukja: 1.3 (CHIN AT RISK, borderline CRACKED; R1 KO loss + defensive decay)
- Layoff cardio modifier: 1.1 (ring rust amplifies late-fight vulnerability)
- Adjusted KO prob (Barbosa): 0.65 × 0.52 × 1.3 × 1.0 = **~0.44 (44%)**

- Buzukja base KO rate: ~25% of total fights (1 KO win in recent 6 fights, 4.21 SLpM career)
- Barbosa Str. Def.: 50% → (1 − 0.50) = 0.50
- Chin modifier for Barbosa: 1.0 (no KO loss history in data provided)
- Adjusted KO prob (Buzukja): 0.25 × 0.50 × 1.0 = **~0.125 (12.5%)**

- Decision prob ≈ 1 − 0.44 − 0.125 − 0.00 − 0.00 = **~0.435 (43.5%)** (no sub threats from either fighter)

Note: Decision probability here is inflated due to LOW data tier on Barbosa and the conservative KO estimate. In practice, 2 FORWARDs with no grappling game in a KO-finish-heavy matchup should push decision probability lower — adjusting down to ~30% decision, redistributing ~13% to KO finishes.

---

**#1 — Marcio Barbosa wins by KO/TKO, Round 1 or 2**
Probability: 42%
Why: Barbosa's volume (8.27 SLpM) against a ring-rusty Buzukja (602 days out, 0.4 SLpM in his last outing) creates the conditions for an early overload. Buzukja's 48% strike defense and documented R1 KO vulnerability (stopped at 0:49 vs. Emmers) make him structurally susceptible to Barbosa's pressure style. Both fighters go FORWARD — the opening minutes will be high-pace exchanges that favor the sharper, more active fighter.

**#2 — Marcio Barbosa wins by KO/TKO, Round 3**
Probability: 18%
Why: If Buzukja survives early rounds by moving, counterpunching, or clinching effectively, the layoff cardio penalty kicks in by Round 3. A 602-day inactive fighter against elite volume output is a recipe for late-round deterioration. Barbosa's volume remains consistent (single-fight KO in R1 doesn't establish a late-finish pattern, but cumulative damage makes R3 viable).

**#3 — Dennis Buzukja wins by KO/TKO, Round 2 or 3**
Probability: 13%
Why: Buzukja demonstrated legitimate KO power (R3 finish vs. Matthews), and Barbosa absorbs 7.56 SLpM in his one UFC fight — he walks into shots. If Buzukja uses his switch stance and reach effectively to land a clean counter early, there is a realistic path to a finish. This is the primary underdog path: one clean counter off a Barbosa overcommitment.

**#4 — Marcio Barbosa wins by decision**
Probability: 17%
Why: The structural scenario where Buzukja's chip-shot counter-striking frustrates Barbosa's combinations without landing a finishing blow. Given Barbosa's 50% strike defense and the absence of any clinch/grappling control from either fighter, a 3-round striking decision is possible — Barbosa wins it by volume on the cards if neither finishes.

**#5 — Dennis Buzukja wins by decision**
Probability: 10%
Why: If Buzukja completely neutralizes Barbosa's volume through movement, clinch work, and accurate counter-striking (his 43% accuracy vs. Barbosa's 47%), a points win is theoretically possible. However, the layoff, the pace mismatch, and Barbosa's activity make this the least likely clean outcome. Would require a near-perfect performance from a 20-month-inactive fighter.
<!--END-->

<!--BETTING-->
## Betting Recommendation

**1. Tier Label:** LEAN (Rule 13.2)

**2. Primary Pick:** Marcio Barbosa | Confidence: 60% (capped — Barbosa is LOW data tier, Rule 13.4)

---

**3. Edge Breakdown:**

*Line conversion (Rule 11.1):*
- Barbosa current line: −350 → Implied prob = 350 / (350 + 100) = **77.8%**
- Buzukja current line: +290 → Implied prob = 100 / (290 + 100) = **25.6%**
- Combined implied: 77.8% + 25.6% = **103.4%** (vig: 3.4%)

*De-vig (Rule 11.2):*
- True prob Barbosa = 77.8 / 103.4 = **75.2%**
- True prob Buzukja = 25.6 / 103.4 = **24.8%**

*Assessed probability:*
- Base matrix read: ~68% Barbosa (KO threat + opponent chin risk + volume dominance)
- Intangible adjustment: Buzukja −17% (layoff + CHIN AT RISK) → redistributed to Barbosa
- Raw assessed Barbosa: ~72%
- **Confidence cap applied (LOW data tier): capped at 60%**

*Edge:*
- Assessed prob (capped): 60%
- Market true prob: 75.2%
- **Edge: −15.2%**

**This is a NEGATIVE edge at the current line.** Barbosa is correctly priced — in fact, the market has moved aggressively to −350, which implies 75.2% true probability. Our capped assessed probability of 60% (forced by the LOW data tier) does not clear the market's price. Even the uncapped estimate of ~72% does not beat the 75.2% market true probability.

**Recommendation: NO BET on Barbosa moneyline at −350.**

**Buzukja at +290:**
- Implied prob (de-vigged): 24.8%
- Assessed prob (Buzukja): ~28% uncapped (FORWARD fighter with KO ability, switch stance, counter-striking)
- Edge: +3.2% — marginally clears the 3% noise threshold (Rule 11.3)
- **BUT:** Buzukja carries LOW-tier opponent data (Barbosa) AND a 602-day layoff with CHIN AT RISK — this stacks to a LOW/MEDIUM confidence scenario.
- Rule 13.7: High edge minimum for a PLAY requires ≥60% assessed probability. At 28% assessed probability for Buzukja, this is not a play — it's an underdog value flicker, not an actionable edge.
- Rule 13.6 ("would I bet $1000?"): **No.** A 602-day inactive fighter with a cracked chin and a 0.4 SLpM most recent performance against an active KO finisher — the 3.2% edge is inside the noise given the uncertainty stack.

**Final recommendation: NO BET — pass on both sides.**

---

**4. Stake:** 0 units (NO BET)

**5. Target Line:**
- For Barbosa to become a play: would need to find a price of **−185 or better** (where ~60% assessed prob creates +5% edge vs. ~55% de-vigged true prob). That price does not exist in this market.
- For Buzukja to become a play: +290 barely clears the noise threshold mathematically but fails the confidence and "would I bet" checks. Value would require +350 or better to justify a LEAN given the uncertainty stack.

---

**6. Data Tiers:**
- Dennis Buzukja: **MEDIUM** (4 UFC fights, 602-day layoff → stale data, per Rule 2.3)
- Marcio Barbosa: **LOW** (1 UFC fight only)
- Active confidence cap: **60% maximum** (Barbosa LOW tier, Rule 13.4)

---

**7. Market Read (Rule 10.1 / Rule 10.12):**
- Buzukja: Open +285 → Current +290 (essentially flat, +5 cents)
- Barbosa: Open +315 → Current −350 (massive swing — over 6.5 points of implied probability)
- **Movement direction: Extremely sharp move toward Barbosa.** A line moving from +315 to −350 is not public action — this is sharp/sharp money identifying a mismatch. The move adds credibility to the "Barbosa wins" directional read.
- **RLM: NO** (confirmed in input data)
- The market move validates the directional pick (Barbosa) but has already priced out the value entirely. This is a case where the sharps got there first — per Rule 10.7 (CLV), the window is closed.

---

**8. Hedge Verdict (Rule 12.5):**
- Combined implied: 103.4% → **NO ARB**
- Best Barbosa line: −350 (77.8%) / Best Buzukja line: +290 (25.6%) → sum 103.4% — no guaranteed profit structure.
- Take the value pick on the best line. No hedge recommended.

---

**9. Props Worth Considering:**
- **Fight ends inside the distance (YES):** Both fighters are FORWARD strikers with no grappling. Barbosa finishes in his only UFC fight; Buzukja has a cracked chin profile. Decision probability from Section 8 math: ~27% (adjusted). If a "fight goes to decision" NO prop is available around +130 or better (implied ~43%), there is value on the "finish" side.
  - Estimated finish probability: ~73%
  - If "Fight ends in Round 1" YES is priced around −110 or better: Given Buzukja's R1 KO history and Barbosa's sole UFC finish coming in R1 (4:14), early stoppage is plausible — but this is a 1-fight sample on Barbosa; do not overweight R1 specifically.
  - **LEAN: Fight ends before the final bell (YES) if available at −175 or better** — estimated true probability ~73% vs. implied ~64% at −175 → +9% edge. Apply small stake (0.5–0.75 units). *De-vig required on the actual book line before placing — this is a conditional recommendation.*

---

**10. Fade Alert (Rule 13.8):**
Initial read was directionally toward Barbosa given the layoff, chin, and output gap. After de-vig math: Barbosa's line at −350 has already absorbed all that edge and then some. **No switch required — the directional read holds but the moneyline has no value remaining.** The fade of Buzukja is correct; the execution is "pass" on Barbosa's price, not a bet against him.
<!--END-->

- **DATA TIER: LOW.** Zero UFC fights on record — all striking, grappling, and finishing-rate stats read 0.00/N/A. Analysis relies entirely on pre-UFC record (10-2-0) and available contextual data. Per Rule 2.2, confidence is capped at 60% maximum.
- **Record context only:** 10-2-0 as a pro, fighting at 185 lbs as a southpaw out of 6'2". Win methods, opponent quality, and pre-UFC finishing breakdown are not available in the provided data — archetype cannot be formally assigned per Rule 4.1. Labeling: **ARCHETYPE UNASSIGNED (data insufficient).**
- **Southpaw stance:** Against an orthodox opponent (Valentin), this is a structural edge per Rule 5.4. Valentin's last-5 fight history does not appear to include southpaw opponents — southpaw advantage is live, not neutralized.
- **Reach disadvantage:** Leblanc carries 74" reach vs. Valentin's 77" — a 3-inch deficit. This is below the 4-inch threshold that triggers a formal reach flag (Rule 5.3), but it is a real positional disadvantage when working from distance.
- **Age intangible:** At 34 in the middleweight division, Leblanc is approaching the Rule 9.7 cliff (men, MW–LHW: cliff at 36). Not yet penalized, but on the slope. No formal age-penalty applied at 34.

- **R1 KO loss recency:** vs. Ateba Gautier, July 19, 2025 — stopped at 1:10 of Round 1. Per Rule 6.3, fighters returning from an R1/R2 KO loss carry a **−10% mental discount** in their next fight. This fight appears to be Valentin's next contest.

Strike defense of **32%** is critically low; he is walking into the vast majority of shots thrown at him. Last-3 SLpM: 0.71 avg (1.71, 0.40, 0.00) — **TRENDING DOWN** vs. career 1.13, largely driven by fight-ending stoppages. Per Rule 3.3: SApM trending, Str. Def. at 32% — **defensive decay confirmed.**
- **Grappling: submission tendency, no takedowns.** Career sub avg of 1.3/15min but 0.00 TD avg — this suggests a guard-based submission game rather than a top-pressure wrestling approach. TDD at 55% (below the 60% threshold from Rule 5.2). In his Aug. 2024 loss to Loder, his only sub attempt activity showed Sub/15 = 4.4 before being stopped in R2.
- **Layoff: 273 days** (Aug. 2024 to the data reference point, though the Jul. 2025 fight updates this — the layoff to *this* fight is N/A, but the most recent loss was July 2025). analysis_sections.head2head:
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

The structural picture here is deeply asymmetric, and almost entirely driven by Valentin's CHIN CRACKED flag rather than Leblanc's offensive profile (which is unquantifiable from available data). What we can say clearly: Valentin has been finished by strikes in 2 of his last 3 UFC appearances, including a 70-second R1 KO in his most recent outing. His strike defense sits at 32% — meaning he absorbs roughly 68% of strikes thrown at him — and his SApM of 2.83 dwarfs his output of 1.13. He is not a fighter who pressures opponents into defensive errors; he is a fighter who absorbs pressure until he can find a submission entry, and that path has been repeatedly closed by knockout in his UFC run.

Against Leblanc — a southpaw debut fighter whose archetype is unassigned — the southpaw stance edge is real. Valentin has no documented recent experience against left-handed fighters in his UFC run, meaning the lead-hand dynamics (Leblanc's jab landing as a power shot to Valentin's open side) are structural advantages regardless of Leblanc's specific style. The 3-inch reach disadvantage for Leblanc is a minor negative, but it matters less if Leblanc fights at mid-range or inside — styles that often neutralize reach gaps. The TDD master variable (Rule 5.2): Valentin's TD defense at 55% is below the 60% threshold, meaning if Leblanc has any wrestling component, it would likely succeed — though we have no data to confirm Leblanc's wrestling.

The net intangible delta strongly favors Leblanc. Valentin carries: CHIN CRACKED (−10% KO-return discount, Rule 6.3), confirmed defensive decay (Rule 3.3), 3-fight losing streak, R1 KO in most recent outing, and 32% strike defense. Leblanc carries: UFC debut penalty (−5%, Rule 9.5), unknown layoff, and zero UFC data. On balance, Valentin's confirmed structural deficiencies outweigh Leblanc's uncertainty penalty — the edge belongs to Leblanc, with the caveat that thin data severely limits confidence.

**Flip conditions (Rule 15.1):** If Valentin has fought southpaws regularly in his pre-UFC or regional career, the stance edge narrows. If Leblanc's pre-UFC losses came via chin damage (KO stoppages), the picture changes materially. If the fight is scheduled for 5 rounds (unknown here), Valentin's late-fight sub attempts become more relevant.
analysis_sections.endings:
## 3-5 Most Likely Fight Endings (Ranked by Probability)

**#1 — Julien Leblanc wins by KO/TKO, Round 1 or 2**
Probability: 38%
Why: Valentin's 32% strike defense and two R1/R2 stoppages in his last three fights establish a documented pattern of early stoppage vulnerability. Using the Rule 8.2 framework: even a moderate base KO rate from Leblanc (unknown, estimated conservatively at 35% given 10-2 record with method unknown) × (1 − 0.32 Str. Def.) × 1.4 (CHIN CRACKED modifier) produces an adjusted figure well above 30%. The southpaw angle adds disorientation pressure. Early finish is the highest-probability single outcome.

**#2 — Julien Leblanc wins by Decision**
Probability: 22%
Why: If Leblanc is a volume or pressure striker without knockout power, Valentin's fragile chin still bleeds the scorecards — his 1.13 SLpM output is insufficient to win rounds. A fighter who absorbs 2.83 strikes per minute while landing 1.13 loses most rounds on the cards. Leblanc's southpaw jab finds the open line consistently in this scenario.

**#3 — Robert Valentin wins by Submission, Round 2 or 3**
Probability: 18%
Why: Valentin's only viable path is a grappling-based one — his sub avg of 1.3/15min and his Aug. 2024 fight showing 4.4 sub attempts/15min (before being stopped) suggest a guard game. If Leblanc is a striker-first debut fighter who gets taken down or pulled into a clinch scramble, Valentin can threaten from the bottom or off a scramble. TDD of 0.00 from Valentin means he's not dragging this to the floor himself — Leblanc has to allow it.

**#4 — Robert Valentin wins by KO/TKO (counter or desperate exchange)**
Probability: 10%
Why: With 0 KOs in his last 3 UFC fights and a 56% striking accuracy (career) but 1.13 SLpM output, Valentin is not a KO artist — but a desperation single-strike counter landing on a chin we have no data on (Leblanc's durability is entirely unknown) is a non-zero path. Conservatively priced at 10%.

**#5 — Robert Valentin wins by Decision**
Probability: 7%
Why: Valentin has 0-0-0 in his last 3 UFC fight outcomes (all losses), and his striking volume (1.13 SLpM) is insufficient to outwork most opponents. A decision win would require Leblanc to completely stall out — possible but unlikely given the structural dynamics. This is the least probable realistic outcome.

*Total: 95% (5% residual for unlisted outcomes — draws, no-contest, etc.)*

**TIER: LEAN**
**Primary Pick: Julien Leblanc — Confidence: 58%**

*(Confidence capped at 60% per Rule 13.4 — Leblanc is LOW data tier. Further capped at 58% per Rule 13.5 — debut fighter with no Contender Series tape available. This does not reach PLAY threshold.)*

---

**Edge Breakdown:**

- Line: Julien Leblanc +130 (current)
- Implied probability (positive odds): 100 / (130 + 100) = **43.5%**
- Robert Valentin: current line listed as +147 (note: both fighters showing positive odds — this is a data anomaly, likely a formatting issue where Leblanc opened −172 and has drifted. Taking the current Leblanc +130 at face value.)
- De-vig calculation: Leblanc implied 43.5% / Valentin implied (100/247) = 40.5% → sum = 84.0%

*Note: A sum of 84% on two-sided market is abnormal — suggests either a data formatting error on the Valentin line or extremely thin market. Standard de-vig produces:*
- True Leblanc prob = 43.5 / 84.0 = **51.8%**
- True Valentin prob = 40.5 / 84.0 = **48.2%**

*(If the Valentin line is meant to be −147, then: Valentin implied = 147/247 = 59.5%, sum = 103%, true Leblanc = 43.5/103 = 42.2%, true Valentin = 57.8%. This alternative read is flagged below.)*

- **Assessed probability (Leblanc wins):** 58%
- **Edge vs. de-vigged market (taking +130 read as correct):** 58% − 51.8% = **+6.2 pp**
- **Edge vs. alternative read (Valentin −147):** 58% − 42.2% = **+15.8 pp** — this is above the 12% threshold triggering a re-check (Rule 11.3). After re-check: the assessed 58% is conservative given CHIN CRACKED + R1 KO return + 32% Str. Def., but Leblanc's LOW data tier justifies caution. Edge is likely real in direction, magnitude uncertain due to line data ambiguity.

**Quarter-Kelly Stake:**
- Using +130 line → decimal odds = 2.30
- Kelly = (0.062 × 2.30 − 0.42) / (2.30 − 1) = (0.1426 − 0.42) / 1.30 = −0.2135 → **negative Kelly**
- Using assessed 58% directly: Kelly = (0.58 × 2.30 − 0.42) / 1.30 = (1.334 − 0.42) / 1.30 = 0.914 / 1.30 = **0.703**
- Quarter Kelly = 0.703 × 0.25 = **0.18 units**

*Note: The Kelly calculation is sensitive to the assessed probability. At 58%, quarter-Kelly = ~0.2 units. At 62% (just above the data cap), it rises to ~0.5 units. Stake recommendation: **0.5 units maximum**, reflecting the data uncertainty.*

**Target line:** Leblanc must be priced at **+115 or longer** for the bet to retain +3% edge at 58% assessed probability. Current +130 clears that threshold — value is present if the line is correctly quoted.

**Data Tiers:**
- Julien Leblanc: **LOW** (0 UFC fights, no stats)
- Robert Valentin: **MEDIUM** (3 UFC fights, full last-3 splits available)
- **Active confidence caps:** Rule 13.4 (LOW tier fighter) → 60% cap; Rule 13.5 (debut, no CS tape) → 58% cap. Result: **LEAN only. Not a PLAY.**

---

**Market Read:**

- Opening line: Leblanc −172 / Valentin +150
- Current line: Leblanc +130 / Valentin +147
- Movement: Leblanc has **dramatically lengthened** from −172 to +130 — a 42-cent swing in favor of Valentin. This is a major shift.
- RLM: Confirmed NO per data provided.
- This line movement suggests either: (a) sharp money hammered Valentin hard enough to flip the line, or (b) significant news (injury, weight cut, camp issue on Leblanc's side) moved the market. A −172 → +130 swing is not normal public action — this is almost certainly sharp-informed movement *against* Leblanc.
- **Conflict with stat-read:** The stat analysis (Valentin's CHIN CRACKED, 32% Str. Def., R1 KO return) leans Leblanc. The market has swung aggressively to Valentin. Per Rule 10.11, when the stat-read disagrees with the market direction, apply a −5% confidence penalty. **Confidence adjusted from 58% → 53%.** At 53%, this drops to the border of NO BET territory (Rule 13.2 requires ≥55% for a LEAN).
- **Re-examining:** The market knows something we don't — specifically about Leblanc, whose UFC profile is entirely blank. That blank could be the signal. If sharp bettors have scouted Leblanc's pre-UFC tape and found a weakness the data doesn't show, the line move is credible. **Downgrading to NO BET pending line clarity.**

---

**Revised Recommendation: NO BET**

- **Grappling: submission tendency, no takedowns.** Career sub avg of 1.3/15min but 0.00 TD avg — this suggests a guard-based submission game rather than a top-pressure wrestling approach. <!--HEAD2HEAD-->
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

The structural picture here is deeply asymmetric, and almost entirely driven by Valentin's CHIN CRACKED flag rather than Leblanc's offensive profile (which is unquantifiable from available data). What we can say clearly: Valentin has been finished by strikes in 2 of his last 3 UFC appearances, including a 70-second R1 KO in his most recent outing. His strike defense sits at 32% — meaning he absorbs roughly 68% of strikes thrown at him — and his SApM of 2.83 dwarfs his output of 1.13. He is not a fighter who pressures opponents into defensive errors; he is a fighter who absorbs pressure until he can find a submission entry, and that path has been repeatedly closed by knockout in his UFC run.

Against Leblanc — a southpaw debut fighter whose archetype is unassigned — the southpaw stance edge is real. Valentin has no documented recent experience against left-handed fighters in his UFC run, meaning the lead-hand dynamics (Leblanc's jab landing as a power shot to Valentin's open side) are structural advantages regardless of Leblanc's specific style. The 3-inch reach disadvantage for Leblanc is a minor negative, but it matters less if Leblanc fights at mid-range or inside — styles that often neutralize reach gaps. The TDD master variable (Rule 5.2): Valentin's TD defense at 55% is below the 60% threshold, meaning if Leblanc has any wrestling component, it would likely succeed — though we have no data to confirm Leblanc's wrestling.

The net intangible delta strongly favors Leblanc. Leblanc carries: UFC debut penalty (−5%, Rule 9.5), unknown layoff, and zero UFC data. On balance, Valentin's confirmed structural deficiencies outweigh Leblanc's uncertainty penalty — the edge belongs to Leblanc, with the caveat that thin data severely limits confidence.

**Flip conditions (Rule 15.1):** If Valentin has fought southpaws regularly in his pre-UFC or regional career, the stance edge narrows. If Leblanc's pre-UFC losses came via chin damage (KO stoppages), the picture changes materially. If the fight is scheduled for 5 rounds (unknown here), Valentin's late-fight sub attempts become more relevant.

## Tanner Boser -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** — 10 UFC fights on record, but last fight was Aug. 2023 and layoff is **987 days (~2.7 years)**. Per Rule 2.3, all career averages are **suspect, not predictive**. Stale data discount fully applied.

- **Archetype: Power Striker (FORWARD)** — Career SLpM 4.70 with 52% accuracy; KO/TKO accounts for 3 of 5 tracked wins (60% KO rate). Fights moving forward, hunts the finish. Secondary: none identified — no grappling whatsoever (0.00 TD avg, 0.00 sub avg career).

- **Stat-trend analysis (last-3):** SLpM dropped sharply — last-3 avg ~1.87/min vs. career 4.70 — **TRENDING DOWN severely (-60%)**. Accuracy also erratic (46.5% → 31.2% → 65.2%). This trend is stale (last fight Aug. 2023) per Rule 3.6 but represents the most recent signal available.

- **CHIN CRACKED flag — Rule 6.2 triggered:** KO loss to Ion Cutelaba (R1, 2:05) in Apr. 2023. That's a R1 early stoppage (condition 2 of Rule 6.2). Combined with age 34 approaching the MW/LHW cliff (condition 5) and defensive decay in last-3 (Str. Def. trending data ambiguous but SApM context supports concern). Two conditions confirmed: **CHIN CRACKED.**

- **Intangibles — CRITICAL:** 987-day layoff (Rule 9.2) = **540+ days → −12% win probability penalty**. All career stats explicitly flagged as suspect for projection. No Tapology intangibles data available to confirm or deny injury cause, camp status, or weight-miss history. This is the single biggest red flag in the fight.

- **Win pattern:** 3 KOs and 2 decisions in tracked 10 fights. Decisions tend to go to split/unanimous based on fight history (losses to Latifi, Arlovski, Nascimento all by decision). When he can't finish, he loses close. Not a decision grinder by choice — a finisher whose output has decayed.

analysis_sections.head2head:
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

This matchup is analytically one of the most data-deficient this system can encounter. Tanner Boser brings a known — if heavily stale — UFC profile, while Gokhan Saricam is a complete statistical blank slate. The matrix (Section 5.1) cannot be properly run because Saricam's archetype is UNKNOWN. What can be assessed is Boser's profile against the structural unknowns of an HW-listed fighter making what appears to be a UFC debut at LHW.

Boser's structural profile, when functional, is that of a forward-pressing power striker with genuine KO threat (60% KO rate in tracked wins, career SLpM 4.70) and essentially no grappling whatsoever (0.00 TD avg career, 0.00 sub avg). His TDD sits at 63%, meaning he has moderate resistance to takedowns but no ability to initiate. The CHIN CRACKED flag is the central concern on his side — R1 KO loss to Cutelaba, a 987-day layoff that is statistically the most reliable negative predictor in this ruleset (−12% win probability, Rule 9.2), and a last-3 striking output that cratered to 1.87 SLpM vs. career 4.70. Even accounting for the stale data discount, the direction of the trend at the time of his last fight was sharply negative.

**Net intangible delta:** Boser carries −12% for the 987-day layoff, and CHIN CRACKED status with at minimum a R1 KO return-from-loss discount (Rule 6.3 — layoff penalty is already the harsher of the two, so no double count). Saricam carries −5% UFC debut penalty plus an unquantifiable unknown-archetype penalty. The data gap on Saricam makes a clean delta impossible to state with precision — but the honest read is that Boser's documented negatives are severe and Saricam's unknowns could cut either direction. **Flip conditions (Rule 15.1):** If Saricam's pre-UFC record reveals elite-level competition and a KO-heavy finishing pattern, Boser's CHIN CRACKED status becomes a major liability and the recommendation shifts strongly to Saricam. If Saricam's record is regional-only with no notable wins, Boser's power-striking baseline (even at reduced capacity) may be sufficient. If Boser's layoff was injury-related, the layoff penalty likely worsens, not improves. If the weight class discrepancy (265 lbs listed) is real and Saricam is cutting to LHW, WEIGHT CUT RISK fires and substantially changes the cardio read.
analysis_sections.endings:
## 3-5 Most Likely Fight Endings (Ranked by Probability)

*Note: Probabilities are anchored to Section 8 math where data permits. Saricam's base rates are all N/A (debut, no parsed history), so KO/sub adjustment formulas are run asymmetrically — only Boser's side can be computed with formula structure. All probabilities reflect the extreme uncertainty of this matchup.*

**#1 — Tanner Boser wins by KO/TKO, Rounds 1–2**
Probability: 28%
Why: Boser's base KO rate is ~60% of wins (3/5 tracked). Against an unknown opponent with 0 documented UFC strike defense, chin_modifier defaults to 1.0 (no CHIN CRACKED data on Saricam), and cardio_modifier is 1.0 (3-round fight assumed). Adjusted KO prob: ~0.30 × (1 - unknown str_def, defaulting to 0.50 proxy) × 1.0 × 1.0 ≈ ~0.15–0.28 range. The higher end of the range is used because Saricam has no documented chin durability. Boser's power in the first two rounds has historically been real — his KO wins over OSP, Pessoa, and Lins all came R1–R2.

**#2 — Gokhan Saricam wins by KO/TKO, Rounds 1–2**
Probability: 25%
Why: Boser is CHIN CRACKED (R1 KO loss to Cutelaba, age 34, layoff 987 days). If Saricam has any legitimate striking background — plausible given 11-2 record — Boser's cracked chin and ring rust combine as a high-leverage finish target. The chin_modifier of 1.4 (Rule 6.2) applied to any reasonable base KO rate for Saricam pushes this probability materially upward despite data scarcity. The 987-day layoff further compounds timing degradation.

**#3 — Fight goes to decision (either fighter)**
Probability: 22%
Why: Section 8.4 decision probability = 1 − all finish probabilities. With both fighters' finish rates uncertain or stale, and Boser's recent output showing reduced volume (1.87 SLpM in last 3), a grinding 3-round decision is plausible. Boser has gone to decision in 4 of his last 6 UFC fights. If Saricam is a defensive or grappling-oriented fighter (unconfirmable), the pace may stall.

**#4 — Gokhan Saricam wins by submission or decision**
Probability: 15%
Why: If Saricam's pre-UFC background includes grappling — plausible for an 11-2 HW/LHW — Boser's complete absence of submission defense context (0.00 sub avg, minimal grappling engagement career) creates a one-dimensional exposure. Boser has never been submitted in UFC, but he's also never been tested against a legitimate grappler at this weight. Speculative but non-negligible given data gap.

**#5 — Tanner Boser wins by decision**
Probability: 10%
Why: Boser has two documented decision wins in his last 3 fights (Camur, Nascimento split). His output in those fights was low-volume (2.2 and 1.0 SLpM) but sufficient to accumulate rounds. If ring rust is manageable and Saricam is passive or unpolished, Boser can grind out rounds without finishing. Least likely path given his chin concerns and layoff, but historically supported.

*Probabilities sum to 100%. Remaining ~0% spread across unlisted outcomes (draw, no contest).*

## Betting Recommendation

**1. TIER LABEL: NO BET**

**2. PRIMARY PICK: PASS**
Confidence: 52% (on any direction — insufficient data to justify directional confidence above coin-flip threshold)

**3. EDGE BREAKDOWN:**

*Boser side:*
- Line: Current Boser +118 (note: appears to read as a positive line — interpreted as +118)
- Implied prob (Boser +118): 100 / (118 + 100) = **45.9%**
- Saricam line: opening and current both +130 per data, but if Boser opened -150, Saricam opened at approximately +130 — consistent
- Implied prob (Saricam +130): 100 / (130 + 100) = **43.5%**
- Combined implied: 45.9% + 43.5% = **89.4%** — *this is anomalously low, suggesting either a data entry issue (Boser current showing +118 vs. -150 open is a massive move) or a genuine line discrepancy*
- De-vig (if both sides taken at face value): True Boser = 45.9 / 89.4 = **51.3%**; True Saricam = 43.5 / 89.4 = **48.7%**

*Assessed probability:*
- Baseline read: Boser ~48–52% (CHIN CRACKED + 987-day layoff are severe negatives; Saricam is a complete unknown)
- After applying layoff penalty (−12%) to Boser's baseline and debut penalty (−5%) to Saricam: net adjustment approximately cancels, landing both fighters near 50/50
- Assessed Boser: **49%** | Assessed Saricam: **51%**
- Edge (Boser): 49% − 51.3% = **−2.3%** → NEGATIVE, no edge on Boser
- Edge (Saricam): 51% − 48.7% = **+2.3%** → Inside the 3% noise threshold (Rule 11.3)

Both sides fall below the 3% edge threshold. No bet on either fighter.

**4. STAKE: 0 units**

**5. TARGET LINE:**
- For Boser to have +3% edge: would need assessed prob ≥ 54.3% → requires Boser at +150 or better AND a meaningful upgrade to his data confidence. Not currently achievable given data tier.
- For Saricam to have +3% edge: would need assessed prob ≥ 51.7% at current +130 line → plausible if any pre-UFC fight data confirms dangerous finishing background, but **unavailable to confirm**.

**6. DATA TIERS:**
- Boser: **MEDIUM** (UFC history present but stale — 987-day layoff, last fight Aug. 2023)
- Saricam: **LOW** (UFC debut, zero parsed fight history)
- Active confidence cap: LOW tier on Saricam → **maximum confidence capped at 60%** (Rule 2.2). Even if edge cleared 3%, this fight cannot generate a PLAY recommendation.

**7. MARKET READ:**

## Tanner Boser -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** — 10 UFC fights on record, but last fight was Aug. 2023 and layoff is **987 days (~2.7 years)**. Per Rule 2.3, all career averages are **suspect, not predictive**. Stale data discount fully applied.

- **Archetype: Power Striker (FORWARD)** — Career SLpM 4.70 with 52% accuracy; KO/TKO accounts for 3 of 5 tracked wins (60% KO rate). Fights moving forward, hunts the finish. Secondary: none identified — no grappling whatsoever (0.00 TD avg, 0.00 sub avg career).

- **Stat-trend analysis (last-3):** SLpM dropped sharply — last-3 avg ~1.87/min vs. career 4.70 — **TRENDING DOWN severely (-60%)**. Accuracy also erratic (46.5% → 31.2% → 65.2%). This trend is stale (last fight Aug. 2023) per Rule 3.6 but represents the most recent signal available.

- **CHIN CRACKED flag — Rule 6.2 triggered:** KO loss to Ion Cutelaba (R1, 2:05) in Apr. 2023. That's a R1 early stoppage (condition 2 of Rule 6.2). Combined with age 34 approaching the MW/LHW cliff (condition 5) and defensive decay in last-3 (Str. Def. trending data ambiguous but SApM context supports concern). Two conditions confirmed: **CHIN CRACKED.**

- **Intangibles — CRITICAL:** 987-day layoff (Rule 9.2) = **540+ days → −12% win probability penalty**. All career stats explicitly flagged as suspect for projection. No Tapology intangibles data available to confirm or deny injury cause, camp status, or weight-miss history. This is the single biggest red flag in the fight.

- **Win pattern:** 3 KOs and 2 decisions in tracked 10 fights. Decisions tend to go to split/unanimous based on fight history (losses to Latifi, Arlovski, Nascimento all by decision). When he can't finish, he loses close. Not a decision grinder by choice — a finisher whose output has decayed.
<!--END-->

<!--F2_PROFILE-->
## Gokhan Saricam -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: LOW** — Zero UFC fights. All UFC stats are 0.00 across the board (debut). Per Rule 2.2, analysis is based entirely on pre-UFC record. Confidence is capped at **60% maximum** per the LOW tier rule.

- **Pre-UFC record: 11-2-0** — No fight history parsed from the data provided. Win methods, opponent quality, promotion level, and finishing rate are all **N/A**. This is an extreme data gap. Per Rule 2.1: *"No UFC stats available — analysis based on 0 pre-UFC fights parsed."*

- **Archetype: CANNOT BE DERIVED** — With no fight history accessible, no archetype can be assigned per Rules 4.1 and 4.2. Tagged as **UNKNOWN** archetype. This is not a stylistic label; it is a data-absence declaration.

- **Listed at 265 lbs / 6'3" / 75" reach** — The weight listing is Heavyweight (265 lbs) while Boser is listed at 205 lbs Light Heavyweight. This is a **significant data inconsistency** — either Saricam is dropping from HW to LHW for this fight, or there is a weight class labeling error. This cannot be resolved from available data and must be flagged explicitly. If he is indeed a genuine Heavyweight making a LHW debut, a severe weight cut is implied — **WEIGHT CUT RISK potentially applicable** (Rule 7.3), but unconfirmable.

- **Age: 35** — For an unknown quantity, fighting his first UFC fight at 35 crosses the women's cliff threshold and is within 1 year of the men's MW/LHW cliff (Rule 9.7). UFC debut penalty (Rule 9.5) fires: **−5% baseline**, potentially −10% if facing a ranked opponent (unclear without ranking data).

- **Intangibles: ALL N/A** — No Tapology data. Layoff unknown. Camp unknown. Weight-miss history unknown. Short-notice status unknown. Per Rule 2.1, all of these are explicitly stated as unavailable — none are estimated.
<!--END-->

<!--HEAD2HEAD-->
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

This matchup is analytically one of the most data-deficient this system can encounter. Tanner Boser brings a known — if heavily stale — UFC profile, while Gokhan Saricam is a complete statistical blank slate. The matrix (Section 5.1) cannot be properly run because Saricam's archetype is UNKNOWN. What can be assessed is Boser's profile against the structural unknowns of an HW-listed fighter making what appears to be a UFC debut at LHW.

Boser's structural profile, when functional, is that of a forward-pressing power striker with genuine KO threat (60% KO rate in tracked wins, career SLpM 4.70) and essentially no grappling whatsoever (0.00 TD avg career, 0.00 sub avg). His TDD sits at 63%, meaning he has moderate resistance to takedowns but no ability to initiate. The CHIN CRACKED flag is the central concern on his side — R1 KO loss to Cutelaba, a 987-day layoff that is statistically the most reliable negative predictor in this ruleset (−12% win probability, Rule 9.2), and a last-3 striking output that cratered to 1.87 SLpM vs. career 4.70. Even accounting for the stale data discount, the direction of the trend at the time of his last fight was sharply negative.

**Net intangible delta:** Boser carries −12% for the 987-day layoff, and CHIN CRACKED status with at minimum a R1 KO return-from-loss discount (Rule 6.3 — layoff penalty is already the harsher of the two, so no double count). Saricam carries −5% UFC debut penalty plus an unquantifiable unknown-archetype penalty. The data gap on Saricam makes a clean delta impossible to state with precision — but the honest read is that Boser's documented negatives are severe and Saricam's unknowns could cut either direction. **Flip conditions (Rule 15.1):** If Saricam's pre-UFC record reveals elite-level competition and a KO-heavy finishing pattern, Boser's CHIN CRACKED status becomes a major liability and the recommendation shifts strongly to Saricam. If Boser's layoff was injury-related, the layoff penalty likely worsens, not improves. If the weight class discrepancy (265 lbs listed) is real and Saricam is cutting to LHW, WEIGHT CUT RISK fires and substantially changes the cardio read.

Probability: 28%

**#3 — Fight goes to decision (either fighter)**
Probability: 22%

Probability: 15%

Boser has never been submitted in UFC, but he's also never been tested against a legitimate grappler at this weight. Speculative but non-negligible given data gap.

**#5 — Tanner Boser wins by decision**
Probability: 10%
Why: Boser has two documented decision wins in his last 3 fights (Camur, Nascimento split). His output in those fights was low-volume (2.2 and 1.0 SLpM) but sufficient to accumulate rounds. If ring rust is manageable and Saricam is passive or unpolished, Boser can grind out rounds without finishing. Least likely path given his chin concerns and layoff, but historically supported.

analysis_sections.f1_profile:
## Melissa Croden -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (2 UFC fights in last-3 sample — insufficient for full trend analysis per Rule 2.4; 7-3 career record provides some career-average signal). **Archetype: Volume Striker trending toward Counter-Striker.** Career 4.10 SLpM with 48% accuracy; however, last-2 fights show dramatic volatility (0.47 SLpM vs. Santos, 1.86 vs. Lisboa) — sample too small to derive a reliable trend.
- **TRENDING DOWN — striking output:** Last-2 average SLpM ≈ 1.17 vs. career 4.10 — a 71% drop. Even discounting the Santos loss (where she was clearly outclassed), Lisboa output was only 1.86 SLpM. Against career 4.10, this is a meaningful downward signal, though sample size limits certainty. **Tag: FLAT-to-DOWN on volume.**
- **Defensive profile — genuine strength:** Career 1.83 SApM absorbed — among the cleaner defensive numbers at 135 lbs. Strike defense sits at 53% career (below-average for the class), but the low absorption rate suggests she is not standing in the pocket taking punishment. **FORWARD tag** — her wins come with pressure, not counter-play.
- **Age cliff active:** 35 years old in Women's division — per Rule 9.7, cliff triggers at 33 for women. Two years past cliff = **−10% intangible penalty** (2 × −5%, capped escalation). Cannot confirm additional intangibles (Tapology data N/A).
- **Grappling: limited but not a liability.** 1.02 TD/15min career with 50% accuracy and 66% TDD. Zero submission attempts in UFC career — grappling is not an offensive weapon, just a defensive option. She is not a wrestler.
- **Layoff: 126 days** (last fight Dec. 13, 2025). Standard turnaround — no layoff penalty per Rule 9.2. However, that last fight was a clear 3-round decision loss, which is a mild form of the returning-from-loss intangible (not the same as a KO return, so no formal penalty applied).
analysis_sections.f2_profile:
## Daria Zhelezniakova -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (3 UFC fights in last-3 sample — meets the minimum; one fight is 301 days old). **Archetype: Volume Striker / Pressure Fighter.** Career 4.75 SLpM is the highest output in this matchup, but accuracy at 41% is below average — she throws a lot and connects a moderate amount. **FORWARD tag.**
- **Defensive liability — the critical number:** Career 2.83 SApM absorbed against 68% strike defense. The SApM is materially higher than Croden's 1.83 — Zhelezniakova gets hit more, despite the better strike defense %. This apparent contradiction (high defense %, high absorption) suggests she is absorbing in volume exchanges rather than standing outside them. **Potential chin-decay monitoring flag** (not yet CHIN CRACKED — only 1 KO/TKO-adjacent notable loss, the Perez sub, no KO loss pattern).
- **Submission vulnerability — confirmed:** Lost to Ailin Perez by SUB in R1 (3:52). TDD at 50% is the most concerning stat in her profile — she will be taken down and is a legitimate submission target on the ground. Cross-reference: Croden has zero sub attempts, so this vulnerability is **not exploitable in this matchup**, but it is a profile note.
- **Last-3 output is highly inconsistent:** SLpM of 2.93 (Mullins), 1.29 (Perez), 0.67 (Rendon) — variance of over 4:1 across her last 3 fights. No stable current identity. Per Rule 2.4 and Rule 3.7, with exactly 3 fights in the sample, trend conclusions are borderline — treat as **FLAT with high variance**, not trending in either direction.
- **Layoff: 301 days** (last fight Jun. 21, 2025). Per Rule 9.2: 270–365 days = **−6% intangible penalty**. Moderate ring rust risk. Tapology data N/A — cannot verify if layoff was injury-related (which would increase the penalty).
- **Age: 30** — no cliff penalty. Relative youth is a mild positive in a 5-year age matchup.
analysis_sections.head2head:
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

Both fighters share identical reach (68") and height (5'9"), both are Orthodox, and both are FORWARD-style pressure strikers. Per Section 4.4 (two FORWARDs meeting), expect exchanges rather than avoidance — the pace dynamic will be set by who commits first and whose output holds up. The key differentiator is **who absorbs punishment better over the course of three rounds**, not who has more reach or who can fight on the back foot.

From a style matrix perspective (Rule 5.1 — Volume Striker vs. Volume Striker), this resolves to: **cardio wins.** Croden's career absorption rate (1.83 SApM) is materially cleaner than Zhelezniakova's (2.83 SApM) — she takes roughly 35% fewer strikes per minute. Against a pressure fighter who throws frequently at 41% accuracy, a fighter who absorbs little is naturally advantaged. Croden's strike defense is only 53% career (worse than Zhelezniakova's 68%), but the gap in SApM is real — suggesting Croden creates distance or moves off-line where Zhelezniakova does not. In a mirror-archetype matchup, the cleaner defensive fighter is structurally favored. However, **Croden's striking output has collapsed in her last 2 UFC fights** (average ~1.17 SLpM) — if that reflects her current form rather than matchup-specific behavior, Zhelezniakova's volume advantage at 4.75 SLpM could overwhelm her.

**Net intangible delta:** Croden carries a **−10% age-cliff penalty** (35 years old, 2 years past Women's cliff). Zhelezniakova carries a **−6% layoff penalty** (301 days). Net: Croden is penalized more by intangibles (−10% vs. −6%), meaning a **−4 percentage point net disadvantage** for Croden purely from intangibles. Croden's path to winning: use her superior absorption efficiency, keep the fight at range, land cleaner on her defense, and find a KO window (she has the only recent KO finish in this matchup — Lisboa, R3). Zhelezniakova's path to winning: smother Croden with output volume across three rounds, win a unanimous decision by volume if not by power, and exploit any cardio dropoff Croden shows in R3 (which she showed against Santos, losing a 3-round decision). Neither fighter has shown sub threat in this matchup, so all outcomes funnel through striking exchanges.

**Flip conditions (Rule 15.1):** If Croden's last-2 SLpM collapse is verified as matchup-specific (Santos was ranked; Lisboa was an inferior opponent), switch confidence toward Croden. If Zhelezniakova's layoff was injury-related, worsen her intangible penalty to −15% and push toward Croden more firmly. If the fight is scheduled for 5 rounds (currently unknown format), R3 cardio risk on both fighters would escalate dramatically.
analysis_sections.endings:
## 3-5 Most Likely Fight Endings (Ranked by Probability)

**Applying Section 8 math:**
- Croden base KO rate: 1 KO in last 2 UFC wins = 50% KO rate (small sample; 7-3 record with "1 KO/TKO in last 2 fights" means 1/2 = 50% of recent wins by KO). Career UFC stats don't show total KO wins clearly from win methods data, so using last-2 sample.
- Zhelezniakova KO rate: 0 KO wins in last 3 fights. Career 10-2 with 0 recent KO wins — not a KO threat.
- Croden adjusted KO prob vs. Zhelezniakova: base ~30% (career estimate, conservative) × (1 − 0.68 str. def.) = 30% × 0.32 = 9.6%; chin_modifier = 1.0 (no CHIN CRACKED flag); no 5-round modifier. **Adjusted KO prob ≈ 10%.**
- Zhelezniakova adjusted KO prob vs. Croden: base ~10% (0 recent KOs, not a finisher) × (1 − 0.53 str. def.) = 10% × 0.47 = 4.7%. **Adjusted KO prob ≈ 5%.**
- Sub prob both fighters: Sub Avg. = 0.0 for both. **Sub prob ≈ 0% both directions.**
- Decision prob (Rule 8.4): 1 − 0.10 − 0.05 − 0 − 0 = **85%.**

---

**#1 — Zhelezniakova wins by Unanimous Decision, Round 3**
Probability: 42%
Why: Two FORWARD Orthodox strikers of identical dimensions collapse to volume and pace. Zhelezniakova's career output (4.75 SLpM) is higher, and Croden's recent output has dramatically declined (avg. ~1.17 SLpM last 2 fights). If Croden's current form reflects her true state, Zhelezniakova out-volumes her over three rounds to win on judges' cards. Zhelezniakova's last 2 decision wins (Mullins, Rendon) confirm she can grind out rounds.

**#2 — Croden wins by Unanimous Decision, Round 3**
Probability: 35%
Why: Croden's defensive efficiency (1.83 SApM absorbed vs. Zhelezniakova's 2.83) is the structural edge — she absorbs fewer strikes despite being the older fighter. If Croden's SLpM collapse in her last 2 fights was matchup-driven (Santos was the harder opponent), she may land cleaner at her historical rate (~4.10 SLpM). Her career striking accuracy (48%) outpaces Zhelezniakova's (41%) — accuracy wins over volume in a 3-round fight when both are durable.

**#3 — Croden wins by KO/TKO, Round 2 or 3**
Probability: 10%
Why: Croden's last KO was in R3 against Lisboa (Oct. 2025) and her only recent finish confirms she carries the power in this matchup. Zhelezniakova absorbs 2.83 SApM — she walks into shots. Adjusted KO prob of ~10% materializes if Croden connects cleanly in a mid-to-late round when Zhelezniakova's output begins to wane. Not a primary scenario given the decision-probability math, but realistically the most likely non-decision outcome.

**#4 — Zhelezniakova wins by TKO, Round 3**
Probability: 5%
Why: Zhelezniakova has not finished anyone in her last 3 fights and has zero recent KO/TKO wins — this is her weakest pathway. However, if Croden shows the same cardio dropoff she showed against Santos (losing a close decision after leading early), accumulated volume damage in R3 could prompt a referee stoppage. Unlikely given Croden's clean absorption numbers, but non-zero.

## Melissa Croden -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (2 UFC fights in last-3 sample — insufficient for full trend analysis per Rule 2.4; 7-3 career record provides some career-average signal). **Archetype: Volume Striker trending toward Counter-Striker.** Career 4.10 SLpM with 48% accuracy; however, last-2 fights show dramatic volatility (0.47 SLpM vs. Santos, 1.86 vs. Lisboa) — sample too small to derive a reliable trend.
- **TRENDING DOWN — striking output:** Last-2 average SLpM ≈ 1.17 vs. career 4.10 — a 71% drop. Even discounting the Santos loss (where she was clearly outclassed), Lisboa output was only 1.86 SLpM. Against career 4.10, this is a meaningful downward signal, though sample size limits certainty. **Tag: FLAT-to-DOWN on volume.**
- **Defensive profile — genuine strength:** Career 1.83 SApM absorbed — among the cleaner defensive numbers at 135 lbs. Strike defense sits at 53% career (below-average for the class), but the low absorption rate suggests she is not standing in the pocket taking punishment. **FORWARD tag** — her wins come with pressure, not counter-play.
- **Age cliff active:** 35 years old in Women's division — per Rule 9.7, cliff triggers at 33 for women. Two years past cliff = **−10% intangible penalty** (2 × −5%, capped escalation). Cannot confirm additional intangibles (Tapology data N/A).
- **Grappling: limited but not a liability.** 1.02 TD/15min career with 50% accuracy and 66% TDD. Zero submission attempts in UFC career — grappling is not an offensive weapon, just a defensive option. She is not a wrestler.
- **Layoff: 126 days** (last fight Dec. 13, 2025). Standard turnaround — no layoff penalty per Rule 9.2. However, that last fight was a clear 3-round decision loss, which is a mild form of the returning-from-loss intangible (not the same as a KO return, so no formal penalty applied).

<!--END-->

<!--F2_PROFILE-->
## Daria Zhelezniakova -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (3 UFC fights in last-3 sample — meets the minimum; one fight is 301 days old). **Archetype: Volume Striker / Pressure Fighter.** Career 4.75 SLpM is the highest output in this matchup, but accuracy at 41% is below average — she throws a lot and connects a moderate amount. **FORWARD tag.**
- **Defensive liability — the critical number:** Career 2.83 SApM absorbed against 68% strike defense. The SApM is materially higher than Croden's 1.83 — Zhelezniakova gets hit more, despite the better strike defense %. This apparent contradiction (high defense %, high absorption) suggests she is absorbing in volume exchanges rather than standing outside them. **Potential chin-decay monitoring flag** (not yet CHIN CRACKED — only 1 KO/TKO-adjacent notable loss, the Perez sub, no KO loss pattern).
- **Submission vulnerability — confirmed:** Lost to Ailin Perez by SUB in R1 (3:52). TDD at 50% is the most concerning stat in her profile — she will be taken down and is a legitimate submission target on the ground. Cross-reference: Croden has zero sub attempts, so this vulnerability is **not exploitable in this matchup**, but it is a profile note.
- **Last-3 output is highly inconsistent:** SLpM of 2.93 (Mullins), 1.29 (Perez), 0.67 (Rendon) — variance of over 4:1 across her last 3 fights. No stable current identity. Per Rule 2.4 and Rule 3.7, with exactly 3 fights in the sample, trend conclusions are borderline — treat as **FLAT with high variance**, not trending in either direction.
- **Layoff: 301 days** (last fight Jun. 21, 2025). Per Rule 9.2: 270–365 days = **−6% intangible penalty**. Moderate ring rust risk. Tapology data N/A — cannot verify if layoff was injury-related (which would increase the penalty).
- **Age: 30** — no cliff penalty. Relative youth is a mild positive in a 5-year age matchup.

<!--END-->

<!--HEAD2HEAD-->
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

Both fighters share identical reach (68") and height (5'9"), both are Orthodox, and both are FORWARD-style pressure strikers. Per Section 4.4 (two FORWARDs meeting), expect exchanges rather than avoidance — the pace dynamic will be set by who commits first and whose output holds up. The key differentiator is **who absorbs punishment better over the course of three rounds**, not who has more reach or who can fight on the back foot.

From a style matrix perspective (Rule 5.1 — Volume Striker vs. Volume Striker), this resolves to: **cardio wins.** Croden's career absorption rate (1.83 SApM) is materially cleaner than Zhelezniakova's (2.83 SApM) — she takes roughly 35% fewer strikes per minute. Against a pressure fighter who throws frequently at 41% accuracy, a fighter who absorbs little is naturally advantaged. Croden's strike defense is only 53% career (worse than Zhelezniakova's 68%), but the gap in SApM is real — suggesting Croden creates distance or moves off-line where Zhelezniakova does not. In a mirror-archetype matchup, the cleaner defensive fighter is structurally favored. However, **Croden's striking output has collapsed in her last 2 UFC fights** (average ~1.17 SLpM) — if that reflects her current form rather than matchup-specific behavior, Zhelezniakova's volume advantage at 4.75 SLpM could overwhelm her.

**Net intangible delta:** Croden carries a **−10% age-cliff penalty** (35 years old, 2 years past Women's cliff). Zhelezniakova carries a **−6% layoff penalty** (301 days). Net: Croden is penalized more by intangibles (−10% vs. −6%), meaning a **−4 percentage point net disadvantage** for Croden purely from intangibles. Croden's path to winning: use her superior absorption efficiency, keep the fight at range, land cleaner on her defense, and find a KO window (she has the only recent KO finish in this matchup — Lisboa, R3). Zhelezniakova's path to winning: smother Croden with output volume across three rounds, win a unanimous decision by volume if not by power, and exploit any cardio dropoff Croden shows in R3 (which she showed against Santos, losing a 3-round decision). Neither fighter has shown sub threat in this matchup, so all outcomes funnel through striking exchanges.

**Flip conditions (Rule 15.1):** If Croden's last-2 SLpM collapse is verified as matchup-specific (Santos was ranked; Lisboa was an inferior opponent), switch confidence toward Croden. If Zhelezniakova's layoff was injury-related, worsen her intangible penalty to −15% and push toward Croden more firmly. If the fight is scheduled for 5 rounds (currently unknown format), R3 cardio risk on both fighters would escalate dramatically.

<!--END-->

- Croden base KO rate: 1 KO in last 2 UFC wins = 50% KO rate (small sample; 7-3 record with "1 KO/TKO in last 2 fights" means 1/2 = 50% of recent wins by KO). Career UFC stats don't show total KO wins clearly from win methods data, so using last-2 sample.
- Zhelezniakova KO rate: 0 KO wins in last 3 fights. Career 10-2 with 0 recent KO wins — not a KO threat.

- Zhelezniakova adjusted KO prob vs. Croden: base ~10% (0 recent KOs, not a finisher) × (1 − 0.53 str. def.) = 10% × 0.47 = 4.7%. **Adjusted KO prob ≈ 5%.**
- Sub prob both fighters: Sub Avg. = 0.0 for both. **Sub prob ≈ 0% both directions.**
- Decision prob (Rule 8.4): 1 − 0.10 − 0.05 − 0 − 0 = **85%.**

Probability: 42%
Why: Two FORWARD Orthodox strikers of identical dimensions collapse to volume and pace. Zhelezniakova's career output (4.75 SLpM) is higher, and Croden's recent output has dramatically declined (avg. ~1.17 SLpM last 2 fights). If Croden's current form reflects her true state, Zhelezniakova out-volumes her over three rounds to win on judges' cards. Zhelezniakova's last 2 decision wins (Mullins, Rendon) confirm she can grind out rounds.

Probability: 35%
Why: Croden's defensive efficiency (1.83 SApM absorbed vs. Zhelezniakova's 2.83) is the structural edge — she absorbs fewer strikes despite being the older fighter. Probability: 10%

**#4 — Zhelezniakova wins by TKO, Round 3**
Probability: 5%
Why: Zhelezniakova has not finished anyone in her last 3 fights and has zero recent KO/TKO wins — this is her weakest pathway. However, if Croden shows the same cardio dropoff she showed against Santos (losing a close decision after leading early), accumulated volume damage in R3 could prompt a referee stoppage. Unlikely given Croden's clean absorption numbers, but non-zero.

analysis_sections.f1_profile:
## JJ Aldrich -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (6 relevant UFC fights available, full splits for last 3, but 413-day layoff triggers Rule 2.3 stale-data caution and Rule 9.2 layoff penalty). **Archetype: Counter-Striker / Clinch-Grinder.** **Tag: BACKWARD.**
- **Volume is the key career stat — but her last-3 tell a different story.** Career SLpM: 3.96. Last-3 SLpM: 1.09 (mean of 1.4, 0.6, 1.27). That is a **TRENDING DOWN (−72%)** signal — dramatic and significant. She is not the same striker the career line implies. This is the most important data point in her profile.
- **Striking accuracy is a modest positive:** Career 47%, last-3 mean ~47% (48.8%, 37.5%, 54.3%) — **FLAT.** She is accurate relative to what she throws, but she throws very little. The Veronica Hardy fight (0.6 SLpM, 37.5% acc) was a near-statistical-zero output.
- **Grappling is sparse but functional defensively:** 67% TD defense is passable. She attempted 0 submissions across all tracked fights and only averaged 0.75 TD/15min career — she is not a grappler offensively. Last-3 shows 1.0 TD/15min vs Andrea Lee, otherwise flat.
- **6-of-7 recent fights went to decision, all 3 rounds.** She has not finished anyone since R2 KO of Liang Na (a lower-tier opponent). Her win method is accumulation and decision — she wins by not losing as much as by engaging. Her path to victory requires judges to reward her activity (or lack of).
- **Layoff: 413 days.** Per Rule 9.2, this is a **−10% win probability penalty** (365–540 days tier). All career stats must be treated as suspect. Her last meaningful performance was 413 days prior to this bout — ring rust is a genuine concern, not a narrative one.
analysis_sections.f2_profile:
## Jamey-Lyn Horth -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (6 UFC fights, full last-3 splits, active, but only 6 UFC fights total and last-3 shows extreme variance across performances). **Archetype: Power Striker / Volume Burst.** **Tag: FORWARD.**
- **Striking output is wildly inconsistent across fights — but the ceiling is elite.** Last-3 SLpM: 7.68 vs Bleda, 2.33 vs Demopoulos, 0.27 vs Maverick. Career SLpM 3.63. The Maverick fight (0.27 SLpM, 21.1% acc) was a near-shutdown performance — likely Maverick's wrestling suppressing all output. The Bleda fight (7.68 SLpM, 72.7% acc) is elite-tier. Her output is heavily opponent-dependent. **Trend: FLAT overall, but bimodal — she explodes against strikers, disappears against wrestlers.**
- **Strike defense is a genuine strength:** Career SApM 2.50 absorbed/min (vs Aldrich's 4.32 absorbed). Str. Def. 63% career. She absorbs significantly less punishment than Aldrich on average — this is a structural defensive edge.
- **KO of Tereza Bleda (R1, 2:05) confirms she carries power:** 1 KO/TKO in 4 UFC wins is a 25% KO rate, but the Bleda finish was dominant (7.68 SLpM, 72.7% accuracy in a short fight). Against a striker who doesn't fight back hard, she can be lethal. Against opponents who pressure and engage, she can be contained.
- **Takedown defense is strong at 79%** — significantly better than Aldrich's 67%. Her own TD average is 1.17/15min at 42% accuracy, suggesting she can get fights down if needed but doesn't rely on it. She is primarily a striker who can defend the takedown.
- **Age: 36.** Per Rule 9.7, women's cliff is 33. At 36, she is **3 years past the cliff → −15% win probability penalty (capped).** This is the most significant negative intangible on her side. 126-day layoff since last fight — no layoff penalty applies.
analysis_sections.head2head:
## Head-to-Head: Strengths & Weaknesses

**Style matrix read:** Horth is a FORWARD Power/Volume Striker facing Aldrich, a BACKWARD Counter-Striker/Clinch-Grinder. Per Section 5.1 (FORWARD vs BACKWARD), the FORWARD fighter typically controls the cage and pressure, while the BACKWARD fighter wins on counter efficiency. The decisive variable is whether Aldrich's counter-striking has enough output to matter — and her last-3 data (1.09 SLpM average) says it does not. The structure nominally favors a pressure striker against a low-output counter fighter who doesn't generate meaningful return volume. Additionally, TDD: Aldrich 67% vs Horth 79% — Horth has the defensive grappling edge, but neither fighter is a takedown threat against the other. This matchup resolves almost entirely in striking.

**The critical exchange: can Aldrich survive and accumulate?** Horth's power and accuracy (55% career, 72.7% in her most recent finish) is real. Aldrich's SApM at 4.32 (career) — the highest of the two — means she absorbs more than Horth in every exchange. However, Horth's own SApM of 2.50 and the Maverick shutout (0.27 SLpM against a pressure wrestler) reveal a fighter whose offense collapses when she cannot operate at range. Aldrich is not a wrestler, so she won't replicate Maverick's gameplan — but Aldrich's general non-engagement (1.09 SLpM last-3) might actually neutralize Horth's explosiveness simply by denying exchanges. The fight likely becomes a tactical, low-output chess match — which historically Aldrich has won by decision and Horth has lost (see Maverick loss).

**Net intangible delta:** Aldrich carries a −10% layoff penalty (413 days, Rule 9.2). Horth carries a −15% age cliff penalty (36 years old, 3 years past women's cliff, Rule 9.7), capped at −15%. Both fighters have significant intangible discounts, but Horth's age penalty is larger. Net delta favors Aldrich slightly on intangibles (−10% vs −15%), though Horth's recent activity (126-day layoff) partially offsets this. Flip conditions: if Horth's aggression generates 5+ SLpM at meaningful accuracy, she likely wins by stoppage. ## JJ Aldrich -- Style & Profile

- **Volume is the key career stat — but her last-3 tell a different story.** Career SLpM: 3.96. Last-3 SLpM: 1.09 (mean of 1.4, 0.6, 1.27). That is a **TRENDING DOWN (−72%)** signal — dramatic and significant. She is not the same striker the career line implies. This is the most important data point in her profile.
- **Striking accuracy is a modest positive:** Career 47%, last-3 mean ~47% (48.8%, 37.5%, 54.3%) — **FLAT.** She is accurate relative to what she throws, but she throws very little. The Veronica Hardy fight (0.6 SLpM, 37.5% acc) was a near-statistical-zero output.
- **Grappling is sparse but functional defensively:** 67% TD defense is passable. She attempted 0 submissions across all tracked fights and only averaged 0.75 TD/15min career — she is not a grappler offensively. Last-3 shows 1.0 TD/15min vs Andrea Lee, otherwise flat.
- **6-of-7 recent fights went to decision, all 3 rounds.** She has not finished anyone since R2 KO of Liang Na (a lower-tier opponent). Her win method is accumulation and decision — she wins by not losing as much as by engaging. Her path to victory requires judges to reward her activity (or lack of).
- **Layoff: 413 days.** Per Rule 9.2, this is a **−10% win probability penalty** (365–540 days tier). All career stats must be treated as suspect. Her last meaningful performance was 413 days prior to this bout — ring rust is a genuine concern, not a narrative one.

<!--END-->

<!--F2_PROFILE-->
## Jamey-Lyn Horth -- Style & Profile

- **Data Tier: MEDIUM** (6 UFC fights, full last-3 splits, active, but only 6 UFC fights total and last-3 shows extreme variance across performances). **Archetype: Power Striker / Volume Burst.** **Tag: FORWARD.**

The Maverick fight (0.27 SLpM, 21.1% acc) was a near-shutdown performance — likely Maverick's wrestling suppressing all output. The Bleda fight (7.68 SLpM, 72.7% acc) is elite-tier. - **Strike defense is a genuine strength:** Career SApM 2.50 absorbed/min (vs Aldrich's 4.32 absorbed). Str. Def. 63% career. She absorbs significantly less punishment than Aldrich on average — this is a structural defensive edge.

Against a striker who doesn't fight back hard, she can be lethal. Against opponents who pressure and engage, she can be contained.
- **Takedown defense is strong at 79%** — significantly better than Aldrich's 67%. Her own TD average is 1.17/15min at 42% accuracy, suggesting she can get fights down if needed but doesn't rely on it. She is primarily a striker who can defend the takedown.
- **Age: 36.** Per Rule 9.7, women's cliff is 33. At 36, she is **3 years past the cliff → −15% win probability penalty (capped).** This is the most significant negative intangible on her side. 126-day layoff since last fight — no layoff penalty applies.

Aldrich is not a wrestler, so she won't replicate Maverick's gameplan — but Aldrich's general non-engagement (1.09 SLpM last-3) might actually neutralize Horth's explosiveness simply by denying exchanges.
